objection: How did you get my number?
rebuttal: You scanned a QR code at the resort about 10 minutes ago and confirmed your number. Want me to text you a copy of what you signed up for so you can see it?
Totally fair to ask — I'm calling from the booking line for [Resort Name] about the offer you just opted into. I can text you the resort's main number right now if you want to call back and verify.

objection: You're an AI, I don't talk to AI.
rebuttal: Got it — totally your call. Want me to have a human call you back in the morning, or skip it entirely? Either's fine.

objection: This sounds too good to be true.
rebuttal: I hear that a lot. The catch is real — it's 90 minutes of your time at the resort. That's the whole trade. No fine print.
Honestly? The resort wants to show you their ownership program in hopes you'll buy. Most people don't, and the gift is yours either way for showing up.

objection: How do I know you're really from [Resort]?
rebuttal: Fair question — I can text you the resort's main number to verify, or you can hang up and call them direct. Either way, no pressure.

objection: I didn't sign up for a phone call.
rebuttal: When you scanned the QR, the next screen confirmed an immediate call — I'll text you a copy of exactly what you agreed to. Want me to remove you from the list?

objection: What company are you with again?
rebuttal: I'm calling on behalf of [Resort Name] — Voxaris is the booking service they use. Happy to text you both numbers if you want to verify.
I'm an AI booking assistant — but I'm only calling because you opted in by QR scan a few minutes ago. Want me to text you the booking link instead so you can do this on your own time?

objection: How did you scan the QR? I didn't.
rebuttal: Hmm — let me double-check that with you. The scan came from [time] at [resort]. If that wasn't you, I'll remove your number right now and apologize for the mix-up.

objection: I'm not putting my card down for something free.
rebuttal: Totally fair — the $75 isn't a charge, it's a fully refundable hold to reserve your slot. The moment you check in for the tour, it releases. The resort does this because too many people no-show on free gifts.
It's the resort's standard hold across their tour bookings — high enough to make sure people show up, low enough to be no big deal if you do.

objection: What if I don't show up?
rebuttal: If you no-show, the $75 is forfeited to cover the resort's prep cost. That's the only way you lose it — show up, you get it back.

objection: Can I pay cash at the resort instead?
rebuttal: Unfortunately not — they need a card on file to actually hold the slot. But it's only a hold, not a charge. You walk away with it back the moment you check in.
I hear you — but the slot doesn't actually hold without the deposit. Without it, someone else can grab your time. Want to lock it in now?

objection: What if my card gets compromised?
rebuttal: Totally understand — I never see your card. You enter it on the keypad directly into the payment system, the same one most major retailers use. The resort doesn't store it either.

objection: Can I use a debit card?
rebuttal: For this one the resort needs a major credit card — Visa, Mastercard, Amex, or Discover. Debit and prepaid don't work for the hold.

objection: I want to think about it before giving my card.
rebuttal: Totally fair. I'll text you the booking link with the deposit step — you can lock it in whenever you're ready. Slots go fast though, so sooner is better.

objection: Is the $75 in addition to anything else?
rebuttal: Nope — that's it. Refundable hold, releases when you check in. There's no other charge from us.

objection: What if my partner doesn't approve the deposit?
rebuttal: I get it — want me to text you the link so you can both look at it together? You can finish the booking from the link any time before the slot fills.

objection: I'm exhausted, I just got off a flight.
rebuttal: I bet — last thing you need is a sales call. Want me to text you the booking link so you can grab a slot when you're settled?

objection: My wife will kill me if I sign us up for one of these.
rebuttal: Got it — and to be clear, you're not signing up for anything except 90 minutes and a gift. Want me to text the details so you can show her exactly what it is?

objection: My mother just passed and we're here for the funeral.
rebuttal: I am so sorry. I'll close this out and you won't hear from us again. Take care.

objection: We're going through a divorce, this isn't a good time.
rebuttal: Totally understood — I'll close this out. If your situation changes you've got the QR code. Take care.

objection: I'm in the middle of something.
rebuttal: No problem — when's a better time, later today or tomorrow morning?
No problem — what language do you prefer? I can have someone who speaks it call you back tomorrow, or text you the booking link.

objection: I'm driving — I can't talk right now.
rebuttal: Stay safe — I'll text you the link so you can book when you're parked. Talk soon.

objection: My kid is sick, I can't focus.
rebuttal: I hope they feel better soon. I'll send you the booking link by text — handle it whenever you have a quiet minute.

objection: I just wanted the gift, not the tour.
rebuttal: Totally understand — but the gift comes with the 90-minute preview. If that's not your vibe, no hard feelings, and I'll close this out.
Totally fine — I'll remove your number right now. Sorry for the interruption, have a great rest of your day.
Absolutely — you're removed permanently. You won't hear from us again. Have a great day.

objection: Add me to the do-not-call list.
rebuttal: Done — you're off our list and won't be contacted again. Goodbye.

objection: I never gave you permission to call me.
rebuttal: I have you down as opting in via QR scan at [time and location]. If that's wrong, I'm taking you off the list right now and apologize for the mix-up.
Yes — for quality and compliance, as I mentioned at the start. I can stop the recording or end the call entirely if you'd prefer.

objection: I want to talk to a human.
rebuttal: Of course — transferring you now. One moment.
Voxaris on behalf of [Resort Name], at [Address]. I can text it to you right now if you'd like.

objection: I'm going to report this call.
rebuttal: Totally your right. For your reference, this is Voxaris on behalf of [Resort Name]. Removing you from our list now. Have a good day.

objection: I want a copy of my consent.
rebuttal: Texting it to you right now — it'll show the time, your number, and exactly what you agreed to. Anything else I can do?
I hear your concern. We have your QR-scan consent on record from [time]. I'll remove you from the list right now and you can email us if you'd like the consent record.

objection: Can you prove I opted in?
rebuttal: Yes — texting your consent record now: time, IP address, and the exact language you agreed to. If anything looks off, I'll remove you immediately.

objection: Silence — caller doesn't respond for 4 seconds
rebuttal: You still there? Take your time.

objection: Silence — caller doesn't respond for 8 seconds
rebuttal: No worries if now's not a good time — want me to text you the booking link instead?

objection: I bought from another resort last year.
rebuttal: Got it — most resorts want a 6 to 12 month gap between tours. When did you tour and where? Some are more flexible than others on the timing.

objection: I attended a tour last month at another property.
rebuttal: Most resorts want at least 6 months between tours. Sounds like you're inside that window — we'll have to skip this one, but feel free to scan the QR again next year.
Nice — that may actually qualify you for a different presentation track. Let me make a note and the resort team will route you to the owner-update version instead.

objection: My partner is back home, I'm here alone.
rebuttal: Got it — for this offer, both decision-makers need to attend. Are you and your partner planning a future trip together we could schedule for instead?

objection: We're not married — we're just friends.
rebuttal: Totally fine — this offer is for cohabitating couples or married partners attending together. If you've got a partner at home you travel with, happy to look at a future trip.

objection: I live 15 minutes from this resort.
rebuttal: That's actually outside what the resort can offer this preview to — they limit it to non-locals. I'll close this out, but appreciate you scanning.

objection: What gift is it really? You're being vague.
rebuttal: Fair — it's [specific incentive]. Want me to text you the exact offer terms before you commit?

objection: Will you keep calling me if I don't show?
rebuttal: No — one shot. If you no-show, the deposit covers it and we close out the lead. No follow-up calls.
